questions.q: On what date did the Constitution of India come into force?
questions.explanation: The Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950, a date now celebrated as Republic Day.

questions.q: On what date was the Constitution of India adopted by the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly adopted the Constitution on 26 November 1949, now observed as Constitution Day.

questions.q: The Constitution of India is best described as which type of document?
options: The longest written constitution of any sovereign nation
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India is the longest written constitution of any sovereign country in the world.

questions.q: The Constitution declares India to be which kind of state?
options: A sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic
questions.explanation: The Constitution establishes India as a sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic republic.

questions.q: What is the status of the Constitution within India's legal system?
options: The supreme law of the land
Equal in force to executive orders
questions.explanation: The Constitution is the supreme law of India, and all other laws must conform to it.

questions.q: Which body drafted and adopted the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly drafted and adopted the Constitution of India.

questions.q: The introductory statement of the Constitution that sets out its guiding objectives is called the:
questions.explanation: The Preamble is the introductory statement that sets out the Constitution's objectives.

questions.q: How many years are celebrated between the Constitution's adoption in 1949 and its coming into force in 1950?
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949 and came into force about two months later on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: Which annual national observance marks the day the Constitution came into force?
questions.explanation: Republic Day on 26 January marks the day the Constitution came into force.

questions.q: The Constitution divides governmental powers chiefly between which two levels?
questions.explanation: The Constitution establishes a division of powers between the Union and the States.

questions.q: In which year was Constitution Day (Samvidhan Divas) first officially observed on 26 November?
questions.explanation: Constitution Day was first officially observed on 26 November 2015, the date of the Constitution's adoption.

questions.q: Which Part of the Constitution contains the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Rights are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution.

questions.q: Which Part of the Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in Part IV of the Constitution.

questions.q: How does the Constitution describe India in its territorial definition?
questions.explanation: Article 1 of the Constitution describes India, that is Bharat, as a Union of States.

questions.q: Which article lays down the procedure for amending the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 368 sets out the procedure for amending the Constitution.

questions.q: Which two words were added to the description of India in the Preamble by the 42nd Amendment of 1976?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment of 1976 inserted the words 'socialist' and 'secular' into the Preamble.

questions.q: The Constitution provides for which kind of citizenship for the whole of India?
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides for a single, unified citizenship for the entire country.

questions.q: Many provisions of the Constitution drew on which earlier colonial-era statute as a structural template?
options: The Government of India Act of 1935
questions.explanation: A large part of the Constitution's administrative structure was drawn from the Government of India Act of 1935.

questions.q: The original Constitution as enacted in 1950 contained how many schedules?
questions.explanation: The original Constitution of 1950 contained eight schedules; the number was later increased to twelve.

questions.q: Which Part of the Constitution deals with citizenship?
questions.explanation: Part II of the Constitution deals with citizenship.

questions.q: Which language version of the original Constitution was signed by members of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Members signed both the Hindi and English versions of the original Constitution.

questions.q: The Constitution describes the right to constitutional remedies, which protects all other Fundamental Rights, under which article?
questions.explanation: Article 32 guarantees the right to constitutional remedies for enforcing Fundamental Rights.

questions.q: Approximately how many articles were contained in the original Constitution as adopted in 1949?
questions.explanation: The original Constitution contained about 395 articles arranged in parts and schedules.

questions.q: Some provisions of the Constitution, such as those dealing with citizenship and elections, came into effect before 26 January 1950 on which date?
questions.explanation: Certain provisions came into force on 26 November 1949, the date the Constitution was adopted, while the rest commenced on 26 January 1950.

questions.q: The illustrations and decorative artwork in the original handwritten Constitution were prepared under the guidance of which artist?
questions.explanation: The artwork in the original manuscript Constitution was created under the guidance of artist Nandalal Bose.

questions.q: The calligraphy of the original handwritten English-language Constitution was done by which individual?
questions.explanation: The original English manuscript of the Constitution was handwritten in calligraphy by Prem Behari Narain Raizada.

questions.q: Which doctrine, holding that certain core features of the Constitution cannot be amended, was established by the Supreme Court?
options: The doctrine of pith and substance
questions.explanation: The basic structure doctrine holds that Parliament cannot amend the essential features of the Constitution.

questions.q: The Constitution borrowed the concept of a written list of Fundamental Rights chiefly from which country's constitutional tradition?
questions.explanation: The idea of justiciable Fundamental Rights was drawn largely from the Bill of Rights tradition of the United States.

questions.q: The Directive Principles of State Policy in the Constitution were inspired chiefly by the constitutional tradition of which country?
questions.explanation: The Directive Principles of State Policy were drawn from the Constitution of Ireland.

questions.q: Roughly how many parts did the original Constitution of India contain?
questions.explanation: The original Constitution was divided into about 22 parts, each grouping related articles.
